verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est: cum consummasset Jesus sermones hos omnes, dixit discipulis suis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ὅτε ἐτέλεσεν ὁ ἰησοῦς πάντας τοὺς λόγους τούτους εἶπεν τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when Jesus had finished all these sayings, he said unto his disciples,

verses.latin_vulgate: Scitis quia post biduum Pascha fiet, et Filius hominis tradetur ut crucifigatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἴδατε ὅτι μετὰ δύο ἡμέρας τὸ πάσχα γίνεται καὶ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου παραδίδοται εἰς τὸ σταυρωθῆναι
verses.king_james: Ye know that after two days is *the feast of* the passover, and the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc congregati sunt principes sacerdotum, et seniores populi, in atrium principis sacerdotum, qui dicebatur Caiphas:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε συνήχθησαν οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ γραμματεῖς καὶ οἱ πρεσβύτεροι τοῦ λαοῦ εἰς τὴν αὐλὴν τοῦ ἀρχιερέως τοῦ λεγομένου καϊάφα
verses.king_james: Then assembled together the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders of the people, unto the palace of the high priest, who was called Caiaphas,

verses.latin_vulgate: et consilium fecerunt ut Jesum dolo tenerent, et occiderent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ συνεβουλεύσαντο ἵνα τὸν ἰησοῦν κρατήσωσιν δόλῳ καὶ ἀποκτείνωσιν
verses.king_james: And consulted that they might take Jesus by subtilty, and kill *him*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebant autem: Non in die festo, ne forte tumultus fieret in populo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλεγον δέ μὴ ἐν τῇ ἑορτῇ ἵνα μὴ θόρυβος γένηται ἐν τῷ λαῷ
verses.king_james: But they said, Not on the feast *day*, lest there be an uproar among the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem Jesus esset in Bethania in domo Simonis leprosi,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦ δὲ ἰησοῦ γενομένου ἐν βηθανίᾳ ἐν οἰκίᾳ σίμωνος τοῦ λεπροῦ
verses.king_james: Now when Jesus was in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper,

verses.latin_vulgate: accessit ad eum mulier habens alabastrum unguenti pretiosi, et effudit super caput ipsius recumbentis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσῆλθεν αὐτῷ γυνὴ ἀλάβαστρον μύρου ἔχουσα βαρυτίμου καὶ κατέχεεν ἐπὶ τῆν κεφαλὴν αὐτοῦ ἀνακειμένου
verses.king_james: There came unto him a woman having an alabaster box of very precious ointment, and poured it on his head, as he sat *at meat*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videntes autem discipuli, indignati sunt, dicentes: Ut quid perditio hæc?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδόντες δὲ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ἠγανάκτησαν λέγοντες εἰς τί ἡ ἀπώλεια αὕτη
verses.king_james: But when his disciples saw *it*, they had indignation, saying, To what purpose *is* this waste?

verses.latin_vulgate: potuit enim istud venundari multo, et dari pauperibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἠδύνατο γὰρ τοῦτο τὸ μύρον πραθῆναι πολλοῦ καὶ δοθῆναι πτωχοῖς
verses.king_james: For this ointment might have been sold for much, and given to the poor.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sciens autem Jesus, ait illis: Quid molesti estis huic mulieri? opus enim bonum operata est in me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γνοὺς δὲ ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς τί κόπους παρέχετε τῇ γυναικί ἔργον γὰρ καλὸν εἰργάσατο εἰς ἐμέ
verses.king_james: When Jesus understood *it*, he said unto them, Why trouble ye the woman? for she hath wrought a good work upon me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam semper pauperes habetis vobiscum: me autem non semper habetis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντοτε γὰρ τοὺς πτωχοὺς ἔχετε μεθ᾽ ἑαυτῶν ἐμὲ δὲ οὐ πάντοτε ἔχετε
verses.king_james: For ye have the poor always with you; but me ye have not always.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mittens enim hæc unguentum hoc in corpus meum, ad sepeliendum me fecit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: βαλοῦσα γὰρ αὕτη τὸ μύρον τοῦτο ἐπὶ τοῦ σώματός μου πρὸς τὸ ἐνταφιάσαι με ἐποίησεν
verses.king_james: For in that she hath poured this ointment on my body, she did *it* for my burial.

verses.latin_vulgate: Amen dico vobis, ubicumque prædicatum fuerit hoc Evangelium in toto mundo, dicetur et quod hæc fecit in memoriam ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅπου ἐὰν κηρυχθῇ τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τοῦτο ἐν ὅλῳ τῷ κόσμῳ λαληθήσεται καὶ ὃ ἐποίησεν αὕτη εἰς μνημόσυνον αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached in the whole world, *there* shall also this, that this woman hath done, be told for a memorial of her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc abiit unus de duodecim, qui dicebatur Judas Iscariotes, ad principes sacerdotum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε πορευθεὶς εἷς τῶν δώδεκα ὁ λεγόμενος ἰούδας ἰσκαριώτης πρὸς τοὺς ἆρχιερεῖς
verses.king_james: Then one of the twelve, called Judas Iscariot, went unto the chief priests,

verses.latin_vulgate: et ait illis: Quid vultis mihi dare, et ego vobis eum tradam? At illi constituerunt ei triginta argenteos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν τί θέλετέ μοι δοῦναι κἀγὼ ὑμῖν παραδώσω αὐτόν οἱ δὲ ἔστησαν αὐτῷ τριάκοντα ἀργύρια
verses.king_james: And said *unto them*, What will ye give me, and I will deliver him unto you? And they covenanted with him for thirty pieces of silver.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et exinde quærebat opportunitatem ut eum traderet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπὸ τότε ἐζήτει εὐκαιρίαν ἵνα αὐτὸν παραδῷ
verses.king_james: And from that time he sought opportunity to betray him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Prima autem die azymorum accesserunt discipuli ad Jesum, dicentes: Ubi vis paremus tibi comedere Pascha?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῇ δὲ πρώτῃ τῶν ἀζύμων προσῆλθον οἱ μαθηταὶ τῷ ἰησοῦ λέγοντες αὐτῷ ποῦ θέλεις ἑτοιμάσωμέν σοι φαγεῖν τὸ πάσχα
verses.king_james: Now the first *day* of the *feast of* unleavened bread the disciples came to Jesus, saying unto him, Where wilt thou that we prepare for thee to eat the passover?

verses.latin_vulgate: At Jesus dixit: Ite in civitatem ad quemdam, et dicite ei: Magister dicit: Tempus meum prope est, apud te facio Pascha cum discipulis meis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν ὑπάγετε εἰς τὴν πόλιν πρὸς τὸν δεῖνα καὶ εἴπατε αὐτῷ ὁ διδάσκαλος λέγει ὁ καιρός μου ἐγγύς ἐστιν πρὸς σὲ ποιῶ τὸ πάσχα μετὰ τῶν μαθητῶν μου
verses.king_james: And he said, Go into the city to such a man, and say unto him, The Master saith, My time is at hand; I will keep the passover at thy house with my disciples.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et fecerunt discipuli sicut constituit illis Jesus, et paraverunt Pascha.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐποίησαν οἱ μαθηταὶ ὡς συνέταξεν αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ ἡτοίμασαν τὸ πάσχα
verses.king_james: And the disciples did as Jesus had appointed them; and they made ready the passover.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vespere autem facto, discumbebat cum duodecim discipulis suis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὀψίας δὲ γενομένης ἀνέκειτο μετὰ τῶν δώδεκα
verses.king_james: Now when the even was come, he sat down with the twelve.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et edentibus illis, dixit: Amen dico vobis, quia unus vestrum me traditurus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐσθιόντων αὐτῶν εἶπεν ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι εἷς ἐξ ὑμῶν παραδώσει με
verses.king_james: And as they did eat, he said, Verily I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et contristati valde, cœperunt singuli dicere: Numquid ego sum Domine?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ λυπούμενοι σφόδρα ἤρξαντο λέγειν αὐτῷ ἕκαστος αὐτῶν μήτι ἐγώ εἰμι κύριε
verses.king_james: And they were exceeding sorrowful, and began every one of them to say unto him, Lord, is it I?

verses.latin_vulgate: At ipse respondens, ait: Qui intingit mecum manum in paropside, hic me tradet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν ὁ ἐμβάψας μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ ἐν τῷ τρυβλίῳ τὴν χεῖρα οὗτός με παραδώσει
verses.king_james: And he answered and said, He that dippeth *his* hand with me in the dish, the same shall betray me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Filius quidem hominis vadit, sicut scriptum est de illo: væ autem homini illi, per quem Filius hominis tradetur! bonum erat ei, si natus non fuisset homo ille.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ μὲν υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ὑπάγει καθὼς γέγραπται περὶ αὐτοῦ οὐαὶ δὲ τῷ ἀνθρώπῳ ἐκείνῳ δι᾽ οὗ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου παραδίδοται καλὸν ἦν αὐτῷ εἰ οὐκ ἐγεννήθη ὁ ἄνθρωπος ἐκεῖνος
verses.king_james: The Son of man goeth as it is written of him: but woe unto that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! it had been good for that man if he had not been born.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Judas, qui tradidit eum, dixit: Numquid ego sum Rabbi? Ait illi: Tu dixisti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ἰούδας ὁ παραδιδοὺς αὐτὸν εἶπεν μήτι ἐγώ εἰμι ῥαββί λέγει αὐτῷ σὺ εἶπας
verses.king_james: Then Judas, which betrayed him, answered and said, Master, is it I? He said unto him, Thou hast said.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cœnantibus autem eis, accepit Jesus panem, et benedixit, ac fregit, deditque discipulis suis, et ait: Accipite, et comedite: hoc est corpus meum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐσθιόντων δὲ αὐτῶν λαβὼν ὁ ἰησοῦς τὸν ἄρτον καὶ εὐλογήσας ἔκλασεν καὶ ἐδίδου τοῖς μαθηταῖς καὶ εἶπεν λάβετε φάγετε τοῦτό ἐστιν τὸ σῶμά μου
verses.king_james: And as they were eating, Jesus took bread, and blessed *it*, and brake *it*, and gave *it* to the disciples, and said, Take, eat; this is my body.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et accipiens calicem, gratias egit: et dedit illis, dicens: Bibite ex hoc omnes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ λαβὼν τὸ ποτήριον καὶ εὐχαριστήσας ἔδωκεν αὐτοῖς λέγων πίετε ἐξ αὐτοῦ πάντες
verses.king_james: And he took the cup, and gave thanks, and gave *it* to them, saying, Drink ye all of it;

verses.latin_vulgate: Hic est enim sanguis meus novi testamenti, qui pro multis effundetur in remissionem peccatorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτο γάρ ἐστιν τὸ αἷμά μου τὸ τῆς καινῆς διαθήκης τὸ περὶ πολλῶν ἐκχυνόμενον εἰς ἄφεσιν ἁμαρτιῶν
verses.king_james: For this is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many for the remission of sins.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dico autem vobis: non bibam amodo de hoc genimine vitis usque in diem illum, cum illud bibam vobiscum novum in regno Patris mei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγω δὲ ὑμῖν ὅτι οὐ μὴ πίω ἀπ᾽ ἄρτι ἐκ τούτου τοῦ γεννήματος τῆς ἀμπέλου ἕως τῆς ἡμέρας ἐκείνης ὅταν αὐτὸ πίνω μεθ᾽ ὑμῶν καινὸν ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ τοῦ πατρός μου
verses.king_james: But I say unto you, I will not drink henceforth of this fruit of the vine, until that day when I drink it new with you in my Father's kingdom.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hymno dicto, exierunt in montem Oliveti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὑμνήσαντες ἐξῆλθον εἰς τὸ ὄρος τῶν ἐλαιῶν
verses.king_james: And when they had sung an hymn, they went out into the mount of Olives.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc dicit illis Jesus: Omnes vos scandalum patiemini in me in ista nocte. Scriptum est enim: Percutiam pastorem, et dispergentur oves gregis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς πάντες ὑμεῖς σκανδαλισθήσεσθε ἐν ἐμοὶ ἐν τῇ νυκτὶ ταύτῃ γέγραπται γάρ πατάξω τὸν ποιμένα καὶ διασκορπισθήσεται τὰ πρόβατα τῆς ποίμνης
verses.king_james: Then saith Jesus unto them, All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock shall be scattered abroad.

verses.latin_vulgate: Postquam autem resurrexero, præcedam vos in Galilæam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ δὲ τὸ ἐγερθῆναί με προάξω ὑμᾶς εἰς τὴν γαλιλαίαν
verses.king_james: But after I am risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Petrus, ait illi: Et si omnes scandalizati fuerint in te, ego numquam scandalizabor.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ πέτρος εἶπεν αὐτῷ εἰ καὶ πάντες σκανδαλισθήσονται ἐν σοί ἐγὼ οὐδέποτε σκανδαλισθήσομαι
verses.king_james: Peter answered and said unto him, Though all *men* shall be offended because of thee, *yet* will I never be offended.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait illi Jesus: Amen dico tibi, quia in hac nocte, antequam gallus cantet, ter me negabis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔφη αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς ἀμὴν λέγω σοι ὅτι ἐν ταύτῃ τῇ νυκτὶ πρὶν ἀλέκτορα φωνῆσαι τρὶς ἀπαρνήσῃ με
verses.king_james: Jesus said unto him, Verily I say unto thee, That this night, before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait illi Petrus: Etiamsi oportuerit me mori tecum, non te negabo. Similiter et omnes discipuli dixerunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ πέτρος κἂν δέῃ με σὺν σοὶ ἀποθανεῖν οὐ μή σε ἀπαρνήσομαι ὁμοίως καὶ πάντες οἱ μαθηταὶ εἶπον
verses.king_james: Peter said unto him, Though I should die with thee, yet will I not deny thee. Likewise also said all the disciples.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc venit Jesus cum illis in villam, quæ dicitur Gethsemani, et dixit discipulis suis: Sedete hic donec vadam illuc, et orem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἔρχεται μετ᾽ αὐτῶν ὁ ἰησοῦς εἰς χωρίον λεγόμενον γεθσημανῆ καὶ λέγει τοῖς μαθηταῖς καθίσατε αὐτοῦ ἕως οὗ ἀπελθὼν προσεύξωμαι ἐκεῖ
verses.king_james: Then cometh Jesus with them unto a place called Gethsemane, and saith unto the disciples, Sit ye here, while I go and pray yonder.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et assumpto Petro, et duobus filiis Zebedæi, cœpit contristari et mœstus esse.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ παραλαβὼν τὸν πέτρον καὶ τοὺς δύο υἱοὺς ζεβεδαίου ἤρξατο λυπεῖσθαι καὶ ἀδημονεῖν
verses.king_james: And he took with him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, and began to be sorrowful and very heavy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc ait illis: Tristis est anima mea usque ad mortem: sustinete hic, et vigilate mecum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε λέγει αὐτοῖς περίλυπός ἐστιν ἡ ψυχή μου ἕως θανάτου μείνατε ὧδε καὶ γρηγορεῖτε μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ
verses.king_james: Then saith he unto them, My soul is exceeding sorrowful, even unto death: tarry ye here, and watch with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et progressus pusillum, procidit in faciem suam, orans, et dicens: Pater mi, si possibile est, transeat a me calix iste: verumtamen non sicut ego volo, sed sicut tu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προελθὼν μικρὸν ἔπεσεν ἐπὶ πρόσωπον αὐτοῦ προσευχόμενος καὶ λέγων πάτερ μου εἰ δυνατόν ἐστιν παρελθέτω ἀπ᾽ ἐμοῦ τὸ ποτήριον τοῦτο πλὴν οὐχ ὡς ἐγὼ θέλω ἀλλ᾽ ὡς σύ
verses.king_james: And he went a little further, and fell on his face, and prayed, saying, O my Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me: nevertheless not as I will, but as thou *wilt*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venit ad discipulos suos, et invenit eos dormientes, et dicit Petro: Sic non potuistis una hora vigilare mecum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔρχεται πρὸς τοὺς μαθητὰς καὶ εὑρίσκει αὐτοὺς καθεύδοντας καὶ λέγει τῷ πέτρῳ οὕτως οὐκ ἰσχύσατε μίαν ὥραν γρηγορῆσαι μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ
verses.king_james: And he cometh unto the disciples, and findeth them asleep, and saith unto Peter, What, could ye not watch with me one hour?

verses.latin_vulgate: Vigilate, et orate ut non intretis in tentationem. Spiritus quidem promptus est, caro autem infirma.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γρηγορεῖτε καὶ προσεύχεσθε ἵνα μὴ εἰσέλθητε εἰς πειρασμόν τὸ μὲν πνεῦμα πρόθυμον ἡ δὲ σὰρξ ἀσθενής
verses.king_james: Watch and pray, that ye enter not into temptation: the spirit indeed *is* willing, but the flesh *is* weak.

verses.latin_vulgate: Iterum secundo abiit, et oravit, dicens: Pater mi, si non potest hic calix transire nisi bibam illum, fiat voluntas tua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάλιν ἐκ δευτέρου ἀπελθὼν προσηύξατο λέγων πάτερ μου εἰ οὐ δύναται τοῦτο τὸ ποτὴριον παρελθεῖν ἀπ᾽ ἐμοῦ ἐὰν μὴ αὐτὸ πίω γενηθήτω τὸ θέλημά σου
verses.king_james: He went away again the second time, and prayed, saying, O my Father, if this cup may not pass away from me, except I drink it, thy will be done.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venit iterum, et invenit eos dormientes: erant enim oculi eorum gravati.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐλθὼν εὑρίσκει αὐτοὺς πάλιν καθεύδοντας ἦσαν γὰρ αὐτῶν οἱ ὀφθαλμοὶ βεβαρημένοι
verses.king_james: And he came and found them asleep again: for their eyes were heavy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et relictis illis, iterum abiit, et oravit tertio, eumdem sermonem dicens.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀφεὶς αὐτοὺς ἀπελθὼν πάλιν προσηύξατο ἐκ τρίτου τὸν αὐτὸν λόγον εἰπὼν
verses.king_james: And he left them, and went away again, and prayed the third time, saying the same words.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc venit ad discipulos suos, et dicit illis: Dormite jam, et requiescite: ecce appropinquavit hora, et Filius hominis tradetur in manus peccatorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἔρχεται πρὸς τοὺς μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς καθεύδετε τὸ λοιπὸν καὶ ἀναπαύεσθε ἰδού ἤγγικεν ἡ ὥρα καὶ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου παραδίδοται εἰς χεῖρας ἁμαρτωλῶν
verses.king_james: Then cometh he to his disciples, and saith unto them, Sleep on now, and take *your* rest: behold, the hour is at hand, and the Son of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surgite, eamus: ecce appropinquavit qui me tradet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγείρεσθε ἄγωμεν ἰδού ἤγγικεν ὁ παραδιδούς με
verses.king_james: Rise, let us be going: behold, he is at hand that doth betray me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adhuc eo loquente, ecce Judas unus de duodecim venit, et cum eo turba multa cum gladiis et fustibus, missi a principibus sacerdotum, et senioribus populi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔτι αὐτοῦ λαλοῦντος ἰδού ἰούδας εἷς τῶν δώδεκα ἦλθεν καὶ μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ ὄχλος πολὺς μετὰ μαχαιρῶν καὶ ξύλων ἀπὸ τῶν ἀρχιερέων καὶ πρεσβυτέρων τοῦ λαοῦ
verses.king_james: And while he yet spake, lo, Judas, one of the twelve, came, and with him a great multitude with swords and staves, from the chief priests and elders of the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem tradidit eum, dedit illis signum, dicens: Quemcumque osculatus fuero, ipse est, tenete eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ παραδιδοὺς αὐτὸν ἔδωκεν αὐτοῖς σημεῖον λέγων ὃν ἂν φιλήσω αὐτός ἐστιν κρατήσατε αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Now he that betrayed him gave them a sign, saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss, that same is he: hold him fast.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et confestim accedens ad Jesum, dixit: Ave Rabbi. Et osculatus est eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὐθέως προσελθὼν τῷ ἰησοῦ εἶπεν χαῖρε ῥαββί καὶ κατεφίλησεν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And forthwith he came to Jesus, and said, Hail, master; and kissed him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque illi Jesus: Amice, ad quid venisti? Tunc accesserunt, et manus injecerunt in Jesum, et tenuerunt eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτῷ ἑταῖρε ἐφ᾽ ὦ πάρει τότε προσελθόντες ἐπέβαλον τὰς χεῖρας ἐπὶ τὸν ἰησοῦν καὶ ἐκράτησαν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And Jesus said unto him, Friend, wherefore art thou come? Then came they, and laid hands on Jesus, and took him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce unus ex his qui erant cum Jesu, extendens manum, exemit gladium suum, et percutiens servum principis sacerdotum amputavit auriculam ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού εἷς τῶν μετὰ ἰησοῦ ἐκτείνας τὴν χεῖρα ἀπέσπασεν τὴν μάχαιραν αὖτοῦ καὶ πατάξας τὸν δοῦλον τοῦ ἆρχιερέως ἆφεῖλεν αὖτοῦ τὸ ὦτίον
verses.king_james: And, behold, one of them which were with Jesus stretched out *his* hand, and drew his sword, and struck a servant of the high priest's, and smote off his ear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc ait illi Jesus: Converte gladium tuum in locum suum: omnes enim, qui acceperint gladium, gladio peribunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς ἀπόστρεψόν σου τὴν μάχαιραν εἰς τὸν τόπον αὐτῆς πάντες γὰρ οἱ λαβόντες μάχαιραν ἐν μαχαίρᾳ ἀπολοῦνται
verses.king_james: Then said Jesus unto him, Put up again thy sword into his place: for all they that take the sword shall perish with the sword.

verses.latin_vulgate: An putas, quia non possum rogare patrem meum, et exhibebit mihi modo plusquam duodecim legiones angelorum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ δοκεῖς ὅτι οὐ δύναμαι ἄρτι παρακαλέσαι τὸν πατέρα μου καὶ παραστήσει μοι πλείους ἢ δώδεκα λεγεῶνας ἀγγέλων
verses.king_james: Thinkest thou that I cannot now pray to my Father, and he shall presently give me more than twelve legions of angels?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo ergo implebuntur Scripturæ, quia sic oportet fieri?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πῶς οὖν πληρωθῶσιν αἱ γραφαὶ ὅτι οὕτως δεῖ γενέσθαι
verses.king_james: But how then shall the scriptures be fulfilled, that thus it must be?

verses.latin_vulgate: In illa hora dixit Jesus turbis: Tamquam ad latronem existis cum gladiis et fustibus comprehendere me: quotidie apud vos sedebam docens in templo, et non me tenuistis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ὥρᾳ εἶπεν ὁ ἰησοῦς τοῖς ὄχλοις ης ἐπὶ λῃστὴν ἐξήλθετε μετὰ μαχαιρῶν καὶ ξύλων συλλαβεῖν με καθ᾽ ἡμέραν πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἐκαθεζόμην διδάσκων ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ καὶ οὐκ ἐκρατήσατέ με
verses.king_james: In that same hour said Jesus to the multitudes, Are ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves for to take me? I sat daily with you teaching in the temple, and ye laid no hold on me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc autem totum factum est, ut adimplerentur Scripturæ prophetarum. Tunc discipuli omnes, relicto eo, fugerunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτο δὲ ὅλον γέγονεν ἵνα πληρωθῶσιν αἱ γραφαὶ τῶν προφητῶν τότε οἱ μαθηταὶ πάντες ἀφέντες αὐτὸν ἔφυγον
verses.king_james: But all this was done, that the scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples forsook him, and fled.

verses.latin_vulgate: At illi tenentes Jesum, duxerunt ad Caipham principem sacerdotum, ubi scribæ et seniores convenerant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ κρατήσαντες τὸν ἰησοῦν ἀπήγαγον πρὸς καϊάφαν τὸν ἀρχιερέα ὅπου οἱ γραμματεῖς καὶ οἱ πρεσβύτεροι συνήχθησαν
verses.king_james: And they that had laid hold on Jesus led *him* away to Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and the elders were assembled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Petrus autem sequebatur eum a longe, usque in atrium principis sacerdotum. Et ingressus intro, sedebat cum ministris, ut videret finem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ πέτρος ἠκολούθει αὐτῷ ἀπὸ μακρόθεν ἕως τῆς αὐλῆς τοῦ ἀρχιερέως καὶ εἰσελθὼν ἔσω ἐκάθητο μετὰ τῶν ὑπηρετῶν ἰδεῖν τὸ τέλος
verses.king_james: But Peter followed him afar off unto the high priest's palace, and went in, and sat with the servants, to see the end.

verses.latin_vulgate: Principes autem sacerdotum, et omne concilium, quærebant falsum testimonium contra Jesum, ut eum morti traderent:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ πρεσβύτεροι καὶ τὸ συνέδριον ὅλον ἐζήτουν ψευδομαρτυρίαν κατὰ τοῦ ἰησοῦ ὅπως αὐτὸν θανατώσωσιν
verses.king_james: Now the chief priests, and elders, and all the council, sought false witness against Jesus, to put him to death;

verses.latin_vulgate: et non invenerunt, cum multi falsi testes accessissent. Novissime autem venerunt duo falsi testes,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐχ εὗρον καὶ πολλῶν ψευδομαρτύρων προσελθόντων οὐχ εὗρον ὕστερον δὲ προσελθόντες δύο ψευδομάρτυρες
verses.king_james: But found none: yea, though many false witnesses came, *yet* found they none. At the last came two false witnesses,

verses.latin_vulgate: et dixerunt: Hic dixit: Possum destruere templum Dei, et post triduum reædificare illud.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπον οὗτος ἔφη δύναμαι καταλῦσαι τὸν ναὸν τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ διὰ τριῶν ἡμερῶν οἰκοδομῆσαι αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And said, This *fellow* said, I am able to destroy the temple of God, and to build it in three days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et surgens princeps sacerdotum, ait illi: Nihil respondes ad ea, quæ isti adversum te testificantur?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀναστὰς ὁ ἀρχιερεὺς εἶπεν αὐτῷ οὐδὲν ἀποκρίνῃ τί οὗτοί σου καταμαρτυροῦσιν
verses.king_james: And the high priest arose, and said unto him, Answerest thou nothing? what *is it which* these witness against thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus autem tacebat. Et princeps sacerdotum ait illi: Adjuro te per Deum vivum, ut dicas nobis si tu es Christus Filius Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς ἐσιώπα καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ ἀρχιερεὺς εἶπεν αὐτῷ ἐξορκίζω σε κατὰ τοῦ θεοῦ τοῦ ζῶντος ἵνα ἡμῖν εἴπῃς εἰ σὺ εἶ ὁ χριστὸς ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: But Jesus held his peace. And the high priest answered and said unto him, I adjure thee by the living God, that thou tell us whether thou be the Christ, the Son of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit illi Jesus: Tu dixisti. Verumtamen dico vobis, amodo videbitis Filium hominis sedentem a dextris virtutis Dei, et venientem in nubibus cæli.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς σὺ εἶπας πλὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ἀπ᾽ ἄρτι ὄψεσθε τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου καθήμενον ἐκ δεξιῶν τῆς δυνάμεως καὶ ἐρχόμενον ἐπὶ τῶν νεφελῶν τοῦ οὐρανοῦ
verses.king_james: Jesus saith unto him, Thou hast said: nevertheless I say unto you, Hereafter shall ye see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds of heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc princeps sacerdotum scidit vestimenta sua, dicens: Blasphemavit: quid adhuc egemus testibus? ecce nunc audistis blasphemiam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ὁ ἀρχιερεὺς διέῤῥηξεν τὰ ἱμάτια αὐτοῦ λέγων ὅτι ἐβλασφήμησεν τί ἔτι χρείαν ἔχομεν μαρτύρων ἴδε νῦν ἠκούσατε τὴν βλασφημίαν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Then the high priest rent his clothes, saying, He hath spoken blasphemy; what further need have we of witnesses? behold, now ye have heard his blasphemy.

verses.latin_vulgate: quid vobis videtur? At illi respondentes dixerunt: Reus est mortis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί ὑμῖν δοκεῖ οἱ δὲ ἀποκριθέντες εἰπον ἕνοχος θανάτου ἐστίν
verses.king_james: What think ye? They answered and said, He is guilty of death.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc exspuerunt in faciem ejus, et colaphis eum ceciderunt, alii autem palmas in faciem ejus dederunt,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἐνέπτυσαν εἰς τὸ πρόσωπον αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐκολάφισαν αὐτόν οἱ δὲ ἐῤῥάπισαν
verses.king_james: Then did they spit in his face, and buffeted him; and others smote *him* with the palms of their hands,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicentes: Prophetiza nobis Christe, quis est qui te percussit?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγοντες προφήτευσον ἡμῖν χριστέ τίς ἐστιν ὁ παίσας σε
verses.king_james: Saying, Prophesy unto us, thou Christ, Who is he that smote thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Petrus vero sedebat foris in atrio: et accessit ad eum una ancilla, dicens: Et tu cum Jesu Galilæo eras.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ πέτρος ἔξω ἐκάθητο ἐν τῇ αὐλῇ καὶ προσῆλθεν αὐτῷ μία παιδίσκη λέγουσα καὶ σὺ ἦσθα μετὰ ἰησοῦ τοῦ γαλιλαίου
verses.king_james: Now Peter sat without in the palace: and a damsel came unto him, saying, Thou also wast with Jesus of Galilee.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille negavit coram omnibus, dicens: Nescio quid dicis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἠρνήσατο ἔμπροσθεν πάντων λέγων οὐκ οἶδα τί λέγεις
verses.king_james: But he denied before *them* all, saying, I know not what thou sayest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exeunte autem illo januam, vidit eum alia ancilla, et ait his qui erant ibi: Et hic erat cum Jesu Nazareno.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξελθόντα δὲ αὐτὸν εἰς τὸν πυλῶνα εἶδεν αὐτὸν ἄλλη καὶ λέγει τοῖς ἐκεῖ καὶ οὗτος ἦν μετὰ ἰησοῦ τοῦ ναζωραίου
verses.king_james: And when he was gone out into the porch, another *maid* saw him, and said unto them that were there, This *fellow* was also with Jesus of Nazareth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et iterum negavit cum juramento: Quia non novi hominem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πάλιν ἠρνήσατο μεθ᾽ ὅρκου ὅτι οὐκ οἶδα τὸν ἄνθρωπον
verses.king_james: And again he denied with an oath, I do not know the man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et post pusillum accesserunt qui stabant, et dixerunt Petro: Vere et tu ex illis es: nam et loquela tua manifestum te facit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ μικρὸν δὲ προσελθόντες οἱ ἑστῶτες εἶπον τῷ πέτρῳ ἀληθῶς καὶ σὺ ἐξ αὐτῶν εἶ καὶ γὰρ ἡ λαλιά σου δῆλόν σε ποιεῖ
verses.king_james: And after a while came unto *him* they that stood by, and said to Peter, Surely thou also art *one* of them; for thy speech bewrayeth thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc cœpit detestari et jurare quia non novisset hominem. Et continuo gallus cantavit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἤρξατο καταναθεματίζειν καὶ ὀμνύειν ὅτι οὐκ οἶδα τὸν ἄνθρωπον καὶ εὐθέως ἀλέκτωρ ἐφώνησεν
verses.king_james: Then began he to curse and to swear, *saying*, I know not the man. And immediately the cock crew.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et recordatus est Petrus verbi Jesu, quod dixerat: Priusquam gallus cantet, ter me negabis. Et egressus foras, flevit amare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐμνήσθη ὁ πέτρος τοῦ ῥήματος τοῦ ἰησοῦ εἰρηκότος αὐτῷ ὅτι πρὶν ἀλέκτορα φωνῆσαι τρὶς ἀπαρνήσῃ με καὶ ἐξελθὼν ἔξω ἔκλαυσεν πικρῶς
verses.king_james: And Peter remembered the word of Jesus, which said unto him, Before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice. And he went out, and wept bitterly.
